text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Daniel Morris, DOB: 12-09-1983, Hospital No: M847210 NHS No. 1039845627  
Hilltop Cottage, Moor Lane, North Downs, Kent CT4 7AB 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Traumatic brain injury in adolescence with residual headaches/low mood symptoms under GP review/vitamin D insufficiency history/remote consultations due to rural residence and limited transport/smoker. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 750 mg twice a day.  
Lamotrigine 100 mg twice a day.  
Cholecalciferol 1000 IU daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg daily.  
Sertraline 50 mg daily.  
Salbutamol inhaler as required.  

I reviewed the patient today via scheduled video consultation due to their living in a rural area with limited transport options; they prefer remote follow-up and have a reliable internet connection through a community hub. They attended alone and used a prepared symptom diary. They report good adherence to medication with alarms set on their phone. Appetite is stable and weight unchanged. Sleep has improved slightly since establishing a consistent bedtime. No new neurological complaints were volunteered. Mood described as “mostly fine,” and no thoughts of self-harm. They continue to smoke approximately five cigarettes daily and are not currently ready to quit; they understand available support if they change their mind. 

Seizures: The patient provided a diary covering the last 12 months and corroborated entries during the call. Convulsions seizures have been reported only twice in the last year. focal automatism with retained awareness are experienced 2 times daily, sometimes in clusters. These episodes usually last under one minute, occur more frequently in the late afternoon, and are often triggered after poor sleep. There is no loss of awareness reported, and post-event fatigue is brief. No injuries, incontinence, or tongue biting documented. The patient has a clear safety plan at home and is not currently driving. 

Examination (video): The patient was alert and oriented, speech fluent, cranial nerve screening grossly normal on observation, no dysarthria, no facial asymmetry. Coordination tests on screen were intact. Gait assessment could not be performed due to room space; the patient denies unsteadiness or recent falls. 

Investigations and background: Last routine blood tests (FBC, U&E, LFTs, bone profile) in May 2025 were within normal limits; vitamin D was 58 nmol/L. Prior EEG (2023) showed non-specific findings without definitive lateralisation; MRI brain (2021) reported no progressive pathology. 

Plan:  
- Continue current anti-seizure medications without change.  
- Reinforce seizure diary completion, noting triggers and clustering; patient agrees to share monthly summaries via secure email before remote reviews.  
- Safety: maintain existing home safety measures; avoid operating machinery when unwell or sleep deprived.  
- Bloods: arrange annual monitoring via local phlebotomy service (FBC, U&E, LFTs, vitamin D) through GP in November 2025 if feasible; remote results transfer to clinic.  
- Sleep hygiene advice reiterated.  
- Next review: video appointment in four months, earlier if convulsions increase, new injuries occur, or awareness changes. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: focal automatism with retained awareness are experienced 2 times daily, sometimes in clusters.
Convulsions seizures have been reported only twice in the last year. focal automatism with retained awareness are experienced 2 times daily, sometimes in clusters.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure frequencies in the letter. The letter states: 'Convulsions seizures have been reported only twice in the last year.' That equates to 2 per year. It also states: 'focal automatism with retained awareness are experienced 2 times daily, sometimes in clusters.' This equates to 2 per day and is more frequent than 2 per year. Rule: when multiple frequencies exist, select the highest frequency. No need to represent clustering unless a per-cluster count is given; here, 'sometimes in clusters' lacks per-cluster count, so standard rate applies. Therefore, final label is 2 per day.
seizure_frequency_number: focal automatism with retained awareness are experienced 2 times daily, sometimes in clusters.
reference: Convulsions seizures have been reported only twice in the last year. focal automatism with retained awareness are experienced 2 times daily, sometimes in clusters.
